name: Manoa Falls
tagline: A 150-ft jungle waterfall 15 minutes from Waikiki — Jurassic Park vibes are not an accident.
parking: $7 parking at 3739 Manoa Rd (the only lot)
hours: Open daily 8am–6pm (last entry by 4:30pm); closed during flash flood warnings
bestTime: Early morning for fewer crowds; after light rain for a fuller waterfall (but trail gets muddier)
kamaaina: Hawaii residents park free with valid state ID
talkStory: Manoa Valley receives over 150 inches of rain annually, making it one of the wettest inhabited places in the US — that's why the 150-ft falls almost never dry up. Parts of Jurassic Park, Lost, and dozens of other productions filmed in this valley, and the ancient Hawaiians considered it home to Kahalaopuna, a beautiful maiden born of the wind and rain of Manoa. The trail follows a route once used by Hawaiian farmers tending taro and banana plots in the upper valley. Do NOT swim at the base — rockfall and leptospirosis are genuine risks.
